text: The surgeon begins medial to lateral mobilization, identifying the inferior mesenteric artery. The left ureter is carefully identified and protected. The left colic artery is preserved to ensure optimal blood supply to the colon. The inferior mesenteric artery is clipped after the origin of the left colic artery and then stapled. Medial mobilization is completed, followed by lateral mobilization.

The surgeon dissects adhesions between the uterus and left ovary, followed by mobilization of the upper rectum in the total mesorectal excision (TME) plane. The rectum is prepared for stapling using an Echelon stapler with green cartridges. The Firefly fluorescence imaging of the da Vinci system confirms good blood supply to the rectal stump, indicated by its green coloration. The rectum is then stapled, and the staple line is cut with scissors.

The surgical team removes the specimen from the abdomen through the anus. The anvil is placed, and a purse string suture is applied outside the abdomen. The bowel is returned to the abdominal cavity, and the injured rectal cuff is excised. A 31mm circular stapler is inserted through the anus to create an end-to-end colorectal anastomosis.
